HomePage.heroTitle: Yashil Energiya — Uzbekistan's national renewable energy operator
HomePage.heroSubtitle: Developing solar power plants, micro hydro projects and EV charging infrastructure for sustainable energy supply.

AboutPage.content: Yashil Energiya - powering the green economy

event.description: 2023-02-16  "Yashil Energiya" LLC was established based on Presidential Decree No. 57 of 2023 on accelerating the introduction of renewable energy sources and energy-saving technologies.
By the end of 2023, the capacity of the PV plants installed and operated by "Yashil Energiya" LLC exceeded 20,000 kW.
In order to contribute to the "green" economy, "Yashil Energiya" LLC has established departments of mini and micro hydropower plants and electric vehicle charging stations.
By the end of 2024, the capacity of PV stations owned by "Yashil Energiya" LLC exceeded 48,000 kW.
2025-09-24 Acquisition of a 51% stake in "Yashil Energiya" LLC by the Emirati company "GSU".
2025-09-29 the company transitioned to a new ownership structure and was reorganized as the joint venture "JV Yashil Energiya LLC".
By the end of 2025, the installed capacity of Yashil Energiya's operating assets reached 130,000 kW.
EV charging stations have been successfully launched in the Kashkadarya, Jizzakh, and Bukhara regions.

AboutPage.paragraph1: JV "Yashil Energiya" LLC is a company that specializes in the development and implementation of renewable energy solutions in the Republic of Uzbekistan. We were founded on February 16, 2023, under the Decree of the President of the Republic, No-57.
AboutPage.paragraph2: Ensuring sustainable development and protecting the environment in order to contribute to the energy independence of the country by widely introducing renewable energy sources. Our main goal is to ensure the provision of quality electricity to all social and governmental facilities.

project1.title: Establish solar photovoltaic power plants to provide quality electricity for the buildings of social facilities, government agencies, and other organizations.

project2.title: Simultaneously charging multiple electric vehicles using high-quality electricity from innovative devices.

project3.title: The construction and efficient use of micro hydro power plants in Uzbekistan.

achievement1.description: Service centers in all regions of the Republic

achievement2.description: 130,000 kW photovoltaic power plants are currently in operation

achievement3.description: In 2 000 social facilities, solar photovoltaic power plants have been installed

achievement4.description: The average annual electricity generated is 65,000 MWh / year.

SolarPanelsPage.title: Solar Power Plants
SolarPanelsPage.list: Today, PV Power Plants (PVPPs) are becoming increasingly important energy sources in many countries around the globe. They are:
SolarPanelsPage.listItem1: Does not harm the environment (clean energy source).
SolarPanelsPage.listItem2: Reduces dependence on fossil fuels such as oil and gas.
SolarPanelsPage.listItem3: Help reduce the cost of electricity.

content.description1: Many developed countries (USA, China, Germany) make extensive use of solar energy. For instance, China has the world's largest PVPP.
content.description2: In Uzbekistan, solar energy is also paid attention to, as the number of sunny days per year exceeds 300. This presents a great opportunity for the development of solar power plants, which will help reduce electricity costs and improve energy efficiency in the future.

SolarPanelsPage.systemTitle: PVPP are divided into 3 different main types:

system1.title: On-grid
system1.description: Connected to the electrical grid. The generated electricity is used first, while the system stops operating if the grid is unavailable.
system1.context: For facilities with a stable grid connection
system1.alt: Diagram of an on-grid solar power plant system

system2.title: Off-grid
system2.description: Operates independently from the electrical grid and stores energy in batteries, making it suitable for remote areas.
system2.context: For remote sites and autonomous supply
system2.alt: Diagram of an off-grid solar power plant system

system3.title: Hybrid
system3.description: Combines grid connection and battery storage, offering greater flexibility and reliability.
system3.alt: Abstract diagram of a hybrid solar power plant system

SolarPanelsPage.mapTitle: Information about the current PVPP status of JV "Yashil Energiya" LLC
SolarPanelsPage.chartDescription: JV "Yashil Energiya" LLC has been investing in social and governmental facilities located in the Republic of Uzbekistan since 2023. The diagram shows changes in the total number of PV plants that are relevant to company.

hero.title: Harnessing water for sustainable energy
hero.subtitle: Yashil Energiya develops small and micro hydropower projects using the potential of Uzbekistan’s water resources. The direction combines engineering design, construction, commissioning and ongoing facility maintenance.
hero.imageAlt: Small hydropower plant with a water channel and hydraulic structure
hero.visualText: Compact generation for local electricity supply

benefits.title: Advantages of small hydropower
benefits.description: Small hydropower plants help use local water resources for stable generation without burning fuel during electricity production.

stableGeneration.title: Stable generation
stableGeneration.description: Water flow can support more predictable output when the hydraulic unit is properly designed.

renewableWater.title: Renewable water resource
renewableWater.description: Projects use the energy of water flow and can improve the efficiency of existing water infrastructure.

remoteSupply.title: Support for remote areas
remoteSupply.description: Micro HPPs can complement electricity supply in rural and remote areas where local generation matters.

lowMaintenance.title: Long service life
lowMaintenance.description: With quality construction and regular maintenance, these facilities are designed for long-term operation.

process.title: How a micro HPP works
process.description: The diagram shows the basic energy path: from water flow to electricity delivery to a consumer or the grid.

waterFlow.title: Water flow
waterFlow.description: Water from a canal or watercourse is directed to the hydraulic unit.

hydroNode.title: Hydraulic unit
hydroNode.description: The structure stabilizes water delivery and prepares the flow for the turbine.

turbine.title: Turbine
turbine.description: The kinetic energy of water sets the turbine in motion.

generator.title: Generator
generator.description: Mechanical rotation is converted into electrical energy.

delivery.title: Electricity delivery
delivery.description: Electricity is delivered to the consumer or to the power grid.

services.title: Micro hydropower services

design.title: System design

construction.title: Turnkey construction

commissioning.title: Installation and commissioning

operation.title: Monitoring and maintenance

legal.title: Resolution on developing the micro HPP network
legal.description: Presidential Resolution No. PQ-129 of the Republic of Uzbekistan dated March 28, 2025 provides measures for developing the micro hydropower plant network. The document focuses on rational use of water resources and greater stability of electricity supply for the economy and population.

chargingTypes.description: Different connectors and power levels suit different journeys, from an overnight top-up to a fast charge on the road.
chargingTypes.note: Connector standards and power ratings are indicative for the Uzbekistan market and are confirmed per station.

faq.title: Frequently asked questions
faq.description: Answers to the most common questions about charging with Yashil Energiya.

q1.question: Which electric vehicles are supported?
q1.answer: Our stations support the common standards on the Uzbekistan market — GB/T for AC and DC, and CCS2 for DC fast charging. Check your vehicle's connector type before charging.

q2.question: How do I pay for a session?
q2.answer: Payment runs through the Yashil Energiya app: top up your balance, scan the station QR code and start the session. The final cost is shown when charging ends.

q3.question: What should I do if a station fails?
q3.answer: Stop the session in the app and try another connector or a nearby station. If the problem continues, contact support on +998 55 514 88 44.

q4.question: Are the stations available around the clock?
q4.answer: Availability depends on the site. Opening hours and real-time status for each station are shown on the map in the app.

q5.question: Will the network expand?
q5.answer: Yes. The charging network keeps growing: new stations are planned in more regions and along key routes across Uzbekistan.

q6.question: How do I contact support?
q6.answer: Call +998 55 514 88 44 or use the contacts page. Our team helps with charging, payments and partnership questions.

cta.title: Want a charging station on your territory?
cta.text: Host a station or become a partner in Uzbekistan's growing charging network. Let's talk about the options for your site.

ChargingGuide.title: How to charge an electric vehicle
ChargingGuide.subtitle: A simple step-by-step flow for charging through the Yashil Energiya app, from finding a station to safely finishing the session.

step1.title: Download the app
step1.description: Install the Yashil Energiya mobile app and sign in to manage charging sessions.

step2.title: Top up balance
step2.description: Add funds to your account in advance so the charging session can start without delay.

step3.title: Find a station
step3.description: Use the map to choose a nearby available charging station and plan your route.

step4.title: Connect cable
step4.description: Park near the charger, open the charging port and connect the cable securely.

step5.title: Start charging
step5.description: Select the connector in the app and start the session when the vehicle is ready.

step6.title: Vehicle charging
step6.description: Track battery percentage, charging power and session status in real time.

step7.title: Finish charging
step7.description: Stop the session in the app, disconnect the cable and check the final payment details.

InstallationPage.title: How to become our client
InstallationPage.intro: Yashil Energiya helps social facilities, government agencies and organizations assess whether their buildings can host a solar photovoltaic installation and receive clean electricity under the approved procedure.
InstallationPage.step1: Prepare a formal application on the organization's letterhead.
InstallationPage.step2: Attach basic facility data: address, building owner, roof area, current electricity consumption and responsible contact person.
InstallationPage.step3: Send the application and attachments to Yashil Energiya for preliminary technical review.
InstallationPage.step4: After review, you will be contacted and the date for the site inspection will be coordinated.
InstallationPage.step5: Receive the technical conclusion and next steps for project approval or implementation.
InstallationPage.doc1: Copy of the organization's registration details or official requisites.
InstallationPage.doc2: Photos of the roof, facade, electrical room and meter cabinet.
InstallationPage.doc3: Electricity consumption data for the last 12 months, if available.
InstallationPage.doc4: Contact details of the responsible technical representative.
InstallationPage.templateText: Please consider the possibility of installing a solar photovoltaic power plant at our facility. We are ready to provide access for a technical survey and submit additional information upon request.
InstallationPage.contactText: Send applications and questions to info@yashil-energiya.uz or contact the commercial department.
